category_title: Probationary high school students with severe behavioral challenges and resistance to instruction
minimal_context: The student is asked to read from the history textbook about the Industrial Age and World War I in the classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Please open your history textbooks and read the chapter on the Industrial Age and World War I.

STUDENT: (Tears a page out of the textbook) Why are you teaching us bad kids?

TUTOR: Please leave the book intact and focus on the reading.

STUDENT: (Throws a paper ball across the room) I hate the subject of history. We don't pay attention while reading.

TUTOR: We need to finish this lesson. Keep trying to read.

STUDENT: (Squirts hand sanitizer on the chair and tries to set it on fire) I'm doing an unauthorized science experiment.

options.A: Ask the student to rephrase the history reading into their own words based on what they think it means.
options.B: Sit and read the history textbook aloud to the student, then talk about the topic with them afterwards.
options.C: Tell the student to move their chair away from the corner because they will not be able to focus.
options.D: Explain to the student that you are going to integrate art into the world history lesson as part of the research.
